title: cereus gracilis var. aboriginum
intro: Cereus gracilis var. aboriginum, also known as the Aboriginal prickly pear, is a cactus plant native to Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of the Aboriginal prickly pear include wound healing, pain relief, and the reduction of fever, as well as the treatment of various skin conditions and infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
